directive:
Amend SC4 (Model Transport port) with two changes, both verified this session:
(A) ADD a requirement: the ClaudeTransport / bin/_sdk_bridge.py spawn boundary MUST scrub Claude Code session env vars (CLAUDECODE, CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID, CLAUDE_CODE_CHILD_SESSION, CLAUDE_CODE_BRIDGE_SESSION_ID, CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT, CLAUDE_CODE_EXECPATH, CLAUDE_PID, CLAUDE_EFFORT) before spawning the nested `claude` CLI subprocess. Rationale — empirically confirmed 2026-07-22: bin/plan and every SDK-spawning splock command (/plan, /qa, /implplan, the retry-loop reviewer) DEADLOCK when invoked from inside a Claude Code session because the nested claude inherits these markers; a minimal claude_agent_sdk query hangs with the vars set and completes (exit 0) with them scrubbed. The SDK bridge has no nested-session guard today. Add a regression/conformance test asserting the transport spawn path scrubs these vars (and that a scrubbed-env spawn does not inherit a parent CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID). This is a real Phase-0 seam-hardening item at the transport boundary, not a reservation.
(B) RESOLVE the flagged operator-confirmation in SC4 toward BUILD-NOW: the CLI-dialect sanitize_schema transform ships NOW as a tested, pure, transport-agnostic helper function (proven in the P0 experiment and pinned by the SC10 golden-fixture conformance test), NOT deferred with CodexTransport. Remove the 'flagged for operator confirmation / safe fallback' hedge — the pure helper is in scope for Phase 0.

value.criterion: Model Transport port lands at `bin/_host/transport.py` as `ClaudeTransport`, wrapping (not rewriting) the existing `bin/_sdk_bridge.py` + `sdk_spawners` model-call/spawn logic; `iteration_loop.py` is not touched. (a) BUILD-NOW pure CLI-dialect `sanitize_schema` helper (operator-confirmed 2026-07-22): a tested, transport-agnostic pure function that strips `$schema`/`$id`/`$comment`, recursively sets `required = all properties` and `additionalProperties:false`, rewrites `const` to a single-value `enum`, drops `minLength`/`maxLength`/`pattern`/`minItems`/`maxItems`/`format`, keeps `enum`, then validates the emission against the ORIGINAL strict schema; it ships now and is pinned by the SC10 golden-fixture conformance test (`test_sanitize_schema.py`). The helper is NOT wired into any live transport in Phase 0 — CodexTransport stays deferred; the earlier 'flagged for operator confirmation / safe-fallback golden-fixture-only' hedge is resolved to build-now. (b) NESTED-SESSION ENV SCRUB (operator-confirmed 2026-07-22, hard requirement): a single shared pure helper scrubs exactly eight Claude Code session-identity env vars — `CLAUDECODE`, `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`, `CLAUDE_CODE_CHILD_SESSION`, `CLAUDE_CODE_BRIDGE_SESSION_ID`, `CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT`, `CLAUDE_CODE_EXECPATH`, `CLAUDE_PID`, `CLAUDE_EFFORT` — kept as a named maintained constant `NESTED_SESSION_ENV_VARS`, applied at every `claude`-subprocess spawn boundary: (i) the `SubscriptionClient` query launch in `bin/_sdk_bridge.py`, sitting alongside `_force_subscription_auth` (`_sdk_bridge.py:286-306`) as a second spawn-boundary env guard, and (ii) `ClaudeTransport.spawn_agent`'s effective-env construction before it delegates to `sdk_spawners`. It MUST be an explicit denylist (never a `CLAUDE_*` prefix wipe) so `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT`/`CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA`/`CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` (the host-neutral env contract, `bin/_env_paths/__init__.py:51-53`) survive, and it MUST be a no-op when the vars are unset, preserving the global zero-observable-behavior-change gate (the only behavior change is nested invocations going deadlock→success — a strict capability gain). Verified by `tests/host_conformance/test_transport_claude.py`, `test_sanitize_schema.py`, and a new `test_nested_session_scrub.py` that — via the injectable `query_fn`/`options_cls` seam (`_sdk_bridge.py:496-561`) and a captured-env stub for the `spawn_agent` path, without launching a real `claude` subprocess — sets all eight vars in a parent env, drives the spawn path, and asserts the effective child env excludes all eight (against `NESTED_SESSION_ENV_VARS`), does not inherit a parent `CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID`, that `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT`/`CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA`/`CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR` survive, and that an env without the eight is byte-identical after scrub; existing planner/qa/retry-loop tests stay green. Depends on SC1 (`bin/_host/events.py` vocabulary); SC5 and SC6 still depend on SC4.

A host-conformance suite gates the port: a single pytest selector union runs `test_events.py`, `test_shim_claude.py`, `test_deny_parity.py`, `test_transcript_claude.py`, `test_transport_claude.py`, `test_sanitize_schema.py`, `test_catalog_claude.py`, `test_roster_schema.py`, `test_registry.py`, `test_routing_static.py`, `test_intent_routing_fields.py`, `test_skill_metadata.py`, and `test_nested_session_scrub.py` (all under `tests/host_conformance/`), plus `tests/test_smoke_battery.py`, and CI is green. The suite enforces zero observable Claude Code behavior change on the standard path. Gating relationships: deny-parity gates the SC2 shim `hook-entry` cutover; the sanitize round-trips (`test_sanitize_schema.py`: byte-stable Claude `strip_schema_meta_keys` round-trip plus the proven CLI transform against golden schemas including `plan_v1.schema.json`, with the gpt-5.6 emission validated against the strict original) gate SC4; the catalog contract gates SC5; and the new `test_nested_session_scrub.py` is part of SC4's affirmative regression proof and must pass before the transport port is considered landed. That scrub test guards three failure modes: incomplete scrub (misses one of the eight vars → deadlock persists), caught by asserting the child env excludes all eight against `NESTED_SESSION_ENV_VARS`; over-scrub (strips `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_*` → breaks the child env contract), caught by the survival assertion; and behavior drift on the non-nested path (scrub not a no-op → violates the global gate), caught by the no-op-when-unset assertion that keeps the zero-observable-behavior-change gate honest for the standard path.

value.name: bin/_host/transport.py (Model Transport port + ClaudeTransport)
value.purpose: Model Transport port: `ClaudeTransport` wraps (does not rewrite) `bin/_sdk_bridge.py` + `sdk_spawners`, ships the proven pure CLI-dialect `sanitize_schema` transform (build-now; not wired into any live transport in Phase 0 — CodexTransport deferred), scrubs the eight Claude Code session-identity env vars (`NESTED_SESSION_ENV_VARS`) at the `claude`-subprocess spawn boundary as a nested-session deadlock guard (explicit denylist, no-op when unset, preserves `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_*`), and keeps `_force_subscription_auth` internal; `iteration_loop.py` untouched.

ops.value: splock's Phase-0 portability scaffolding introduces host-neutral seams so the tool can later run under non-Claude hosts and transports without a rewrite, while shipping zero observable Claude Code behavior change today. The plan reconciles: (1) a frozen host-neutral hook event vocabulary (`bin/_host/events.py`) that every downstream port shares; (2) a deny-parity gate that must hold before the Hook Shim `hook-entry` trampoline cutover; (3) the two distinct meanings of 'host' — the RDS 'host session id' flag in the Transcript Provider (SC3) versus the Claude Code session-identity env vars scrubbed at the model-transport spawn boundary (SC4) — which do not collide; (4) a fixed-verifier invariant and roster `schema_version` 4 for ModelCatalog role→pin resolution (SC5); (5) the CLI-dialect `sanitize_schema` transform is empirically proven (a canned gpt-5.6 emission validated against the ORIGINAL strict schema, pinned by `test_sanitize_schema.py` against golden schemas including `plan_v1.schema.json`); and (6) the nested-session deadlock is empirically confirmed (2026-07-22): SDK-spawning splock commands hang when invoked inside a Claude Code session because the nested `claude` inherits eight session-identity env vars — so ClaudeTransport/`_sdk_bridge` MUST scrub `CLAUDECODE, CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID, CLAUDE_CODE_CHILD_SESSION, CLAUDE_CODE_BRIDGE_SESSION_ID, CLAUDE_CODE_ENTRYPOINT, CLAUDE_CODE_EXECPATH, CLAUDE_PID, CLAUDE_EFFORT` at the spawn boundary (a build-now SC4 item, not a reservation; no-op when unset, preserving `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_*`); and the CLI-dialect `sanitize_schema` helper ships now as a tested pure function (the earlier operator-confirmation hedge is resolved to build-now), while CodexTransport stays deferred.
